message:
fix(database): reorder orphaned-txn cleanup, tighten pool return, add Sync bound to ToSql

Three related database-layer correctness improvements:

- postgres.rs: Move clear_orphaned_transaction() calls to after the
  Parse/Bind/Describe/Execute/Sync message buffers are fully constructed.
  Previously the cleanup ran before message construction, creating a
  window where a connection error during cleanup would leave a partially
  built message pipeline. Now the cleanup only runs immediately before
  the write_all() call, ensuring either the full pipeline is sent or the
  error is returned cleanly. This applies to all four query/execute paths
  (query, query_raw, execute_prepared, execute_prepared_raw).

- postgres.rs: Add Sync supertrait bound to the ToSql trait. This is
  required for safe concurrent parameter encoding when connections are
  shared across tasks, preventing potential data races in parameter
  serialization.

- pool.rs: Refactor return_connection() to move the actual disconnect
  call outside the mutex guard scope. Previously the method used a bool
  flag to defer the disconnect, but the connection itself was consumed
  inside the lock. Now the connection is moved out via Option<Connection>
  so disconnect() receives the owned connection after the lock is dropped,
  eliminating any risk of holding the pool lock during potentially slow
  disconnect I/O.

- transaction.rs: Remove unused CancelReason import.
- tests/database_pool_integration.rs: Remove two unused crate::common::*
  imports that triggered dead-code warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
